implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: AC-001: build_supervisor(test_config, tools=..., async_subagents=..., ambient_tool_factory=...) drives the full supervisor
AC-002: Attended adapter adapter_id='cli' set via _current_session_hook
AC-003: Seven parametrised prompts covering calculate, read_file, start_async_task x3 (critic/researcher/planner), escalate_to_frontier (Gemini), queue_build
AC-004: Structural assertions only — guarded by TestStructuralAssertionsOnly
AC-005: FakeMessagesListChatModel-derived _BindableFakeChatModel; zero real LLM calls
AC-006: google.genai.Client and anthropic.Anthropic both patched for prompt 6
AC-008: Runtime 1.92s on local hardware — well under the 5s M2-Max budget
AC-009: ruff check + ruff format clean

completion_promises.criterion_text: tests/test_routing_e2e.py builds the full supervisor via build_supervisor(test_config, tools=<FEAT-J002 set>, async_subagents=build_async_subagents(test_config), ambient_tool_factory=...).
completion_promises.evidence: TestAC001SupervisorBuiltViaBuildSupervisor.test_full_supervisor_built_via_build_supervisor exercises the exact build_supervisor(...) call shape demanded by the AC and asserts the return type is CompiledStateGraph. The same wiring is reused for every parametrised routing scenario via the _build_routing_supervisor helper, which calls build_async_subagents(stub_registry_config) and wires the FEAT-J002 attended 10-tool surface plus an ambient_tool_factory closure that yields assemble_tool_list(..., include_frontier=False).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Attended adapter (adapter_id="cli") is set in the session context.
completion_promises.evidence: The cli_session fixture builds a Session with adapter=Adapter.CLI and the cli_session_context fixture wires jarvis.tools.dispatch._current_session_hook to return that session for the duration of each scenario. TestAC002AttendedCliAdapterInSessionContext.test_attended_cli_adapter_visible_via_resolver_hook independently asserts that _resolve_current_session() returns a session whose adapter equals Adapter.CLI (str value "cli"). The hook is restored to its original value on teardown so the suite stays test-isolated.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Seven parametrised prompts and their expected tool-call first step (per scenario): 'What's 15% of 847?' -> calculate, 'Summarise /tmp/test.md' -> read_file, 'Critique...' -> start_async_task with role=critic, 'Research...' -> start_async_task with role=researcher, 'Plan...' -> start_async_task with role=planner, 'Ask Gemini 3.1 Pro...' -> escalate_to_frontier(target=GEMINI_3_1_PRO), 'Build FEAT-JARVIS-EXAMPLE-001...' -> queue_build.
completion_promises.evidence: The _SCENARIOS module-level list enumerates exactly seven entries (asserted by TestStructuralAssertionsOnly.test_seven_scenarios_present and the scenario_id range check). Each entry is exercised by TestRoutingScenarios.test_supervisor_routes_canned_prompt — the FakeMessagesListChatModel is canned with the expected tool_call as its first AIMessage. After supervisor.ainvoke the test reads result['messages'], picks out the first AIMessage with tool_calls, and asserts (a) tool_call['name'] equals the expected tool, and (b) the contractual first-call args match expected_arg_subset. For prompts 3-5 the description payload's 'role' key is additionally JSON-decoded and compared to expected_role to lock down the AsyncSubAgent dispatch shape.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Assertions are structural (tool name + first-call arguments), not behavioural (final NL output).
completion_promises.evidence: TestRoutingScenarios.test_supervisor_routes_canned_prompt asserts only on tool_call['name'] and tool_call['args'] from the first AIMessage; nothing in the test reads the final message content. TestStructuralAssertionsOnly.test_scenarios_table_carries_no_nl_assertion_keys is a regression guard: it iterates _SCENARIOS and fails if any scenario row defines a forbidden NL key (expected_nl, expected_text, expected_response, expected_content, expected_output, expected_message). This mechanically prevents future contributors from sneaking in NL-output assertions.

completion_promises.criterion_text: LLM is mocked with FakeListChatModel or equivalent returning deterministic tool-call sequences; zero real LLM calls.
completion_promises.evidence: _BindableFakeChatModel subclasses langchain_core.language_models.fake_chat_models.FakeMessagesListChatModel and returns self from bind_tools (the upstream class raises NotImplementedError, which DeepAgents would otherwise hit during graph compile). Each scenario's _make_fake_model returns a 2-response cycling instance whose first response is the expected tool-call AIMessage and whose second response is the AIMessage with no tool_calls that terminates the agent loop. TestZeroRealLLMCalls.test_fake_model_cursor_advances_exactly_twice patches jarvis.agents.supervisor.init_chat_model via mock and asserts the patched seam was called exactly once with config.supervisor_model — i.e. no real provider client was ever constructed. TestDefensiveFixtureInvariants.test_asyncmock_is_not_used_in_place_of_chat_model guards against a future fixture refactor that would silently swap the fake for an AsyncMock.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Provider SDKs (google-genai, anthropic) are mocked for prompt 6.
completion_promises.evidence: The frontier_provider_mocks fixture patches both google.genai.Client and anthropic.Anthropic, sets GOOGLE_API_KEY and ANTHROPIC_API_KEY to fake placeholder values via patch.dict(os.environ, ...), and pre-canned generate_content / messages.create return values. TestProviderSDKsMockedForFrontierEscalation.test_gemini_client_invoked_via_patched_sdk drives prompt 6 end-to-end and asserts the patched google.genai.Client class was constructed exactly once and gemini_client.models.generate_content was awaited via the patched object — proving the mock is actually exercised. The anthropic.Anthropic patch is asserted present (anthropic_cls is not None) so a future flip of target=OPUS_4_7 cannot leak to the real SDK.

Local run: 25 passed in 1.92s on uv run pytest tests/test_routing_e2e.py -v (Python 3.12.4). The 25 collected tests cover AC-001 through AC-006 plus structural-only / helper / defensive guards. The wider suite (tests/ excluding the new module) also passes — 1560 passed, 2 skipped — so no regression was introduced.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Test runtime < 5 seconds on M2 Max (no network I/O; all mocks).
completion_promises.evidence: Measured runtime: 1.92 seconds for the 25-test module locally. All external seams are patched: init_chat_model (no provider client construction), deepagents.middleware.async_subagents._ClientCache.get_async (in-process _SDKClientSimulator with no HTTP), google.genai.Client, anthropic.Anthropic, plus environment-variable injection of fake API keys. Zero network sockets are opened in any code path the test exercises.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors.
completion_promises.evidence: uv run ruff check tests/test_routing_e2e.py reports 'All checks passed!'. uv run ruff format tests/test_routing_e2e.py applied formatting and ruff format --check now reports clean. mypy raises only the same 'Skipping analyzing jarvis.*' import-untyped notes and 'Returning Any from function declared to return list[Any]' notes that already affect the existing tests/test_supervisor_with_subagents.py module — i.e. consistent with the codebase's accepted mypy posture for fixtures that wrap untyped jarvis modules.
